vata: Apply warm sesame oil to your feet before sleep to calm Vata
Eat warm, freshly cooked meals at regular times each day
Sip ginger-fennel tea instead of chilled drinks in the evening
Practice slow Nadi Shodhana for 10 minutes to steady the mind
Add a teaspoon of ghee to lunch to reduce dryness and roughness
Sleep before 10:30 PM to stabilize Vata circadian imbalance
Prefer grounding yoga such as Vajrasana and Balasana daily
Avoid skipping meals, especially during windy or cold weather
Take warm baths with a few drops of calming lavender oil
Include soaked almonds and dates in the morning for nourishment
pitta: Drink coconut water in the morning to cool excess Pitta
Prefer sweet-bitter foods like cucumber, gourds, and leafy greens
Avoid very spicy, fried, and sour foods during peak afternoon heat
Practice Sheetali or Sheetkari pranayama for 8 to 10 minutes
Use coriander and fennel infusion to reduce internal heat
Do moderate exercise in early morning, not in midday sun
Apply cooling aloe vera gel for mild heat-related skin irritation
Keep dinner light and early to support calmer nighttime digestion
Choose moonlight walks or gentle evening relaxation to reduce irritability
Use amla regularly in diet for cooling antioxidant support
kapha: Start your morning with dry ginger and honey to stimulate sluggish Kapha
Do brisk walking or Surya Namaskar for at least 20 minutes daily
Favor light, warm, and mildly spiced meals over heavy dairy-rich foods
Avoid day sleep, especially after lunch, to reduce lethargy
Drink warm water with lemon and a pinch of trikatu in the morning
Use barley, millet, and green gram for lighter digestion
Practice Kapalabhati under guidance to improve respiratory vitality
Limit sweets and cold desserts that increase mucus tendency
Keep a consistent wake-up time before sunrise to reduce heaviness
Use tulsi and black pepper herbal tea during damp weather
